texto: If you don't like the place where you find yourself, Waldemar, it pays to remember that you'll be somewhere else in just a moment. The place itself will be a different place.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Wray: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “If you don't like the place where you find yourself, Waldemar, it pays to remember that you'll be…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, time-travel, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e reflexao.

frase_original: If you don't like the place where you find yourself, Waldemar, it pays to remember that you'll be somewhere else in just a moment. The place itself will be a different place.

texto: My mother and I looked at each other then, full in the face, more frankly than we'd done since I was small. I realized with a jolt that I was taller than she was by at least half a foot. When on earth had that happened? The realization made me want to sit down on the stairs and cry. It seemed to signify something terrible about the world: something that couldn't - or mustn't- be put into words. And I could see, looking down into her startled, anxious face, that my mother felt exactly the same way.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Wray que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “My mother and I looked at each other then, full in the face, more frankly than we'd done since I …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, aging, mothers, com foco em mothers e time.

texto: Harder still was the pretense her studies demanded: the need to dissemble, to parrot her professors' orthodoxies, to feign interest in theories that were of no use to her.

texto: Only a blind man could have lived through these last years without seeing what was bearing down upon us; so I made myself blind as I could manage. I wanted to believe the worst was behind me, and I found an easy way to make it so. I simply turned my back on what was coming.
explicacao: Citação de John Wray sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Only a blind man could have lived through these last years without seeing what was bearing down u…” articula autoestima, history, ignorance em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como ignorance e war.
